UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores non-authentication related profile information for a user, linked to an external authentication system. This includes saved contact details for quicker checkouts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

authSystemId.description: Unique identifier from the external authentication system for this user. This ID links the user's operational data to their authentication context. (Relationship: ExternalAuthSystem 1:1 UserProfile)

fullName.description: The full name of the user, used for shipping and communication.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the user.

address.description: The primary shipping or billing address saved by the user.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile information was updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for products, enabling organized browsing and filtering. Examples include 'PlayStation Redeem Codes' or 'Xbox Gift Cards'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The distinct name of the product category (e.g., 'PlayStation Redeem Codes', 'Steam Cards').

description.description: A brief explanation or summary of what this category encompasses.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a digital game redeem code or a gaming product available for sale on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The official name of the product, displayed to users.

shortDescription.description: A concise summary of the product, typically shown in product listings or search results.

fullDescription.description: A comprehensive and detailed description of the product, including features, compatibility, and usage instructions, displayed on the product details page.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

imageUrl.description: The URL or path to the main image representing the product.

stockStatus.description: Indicates the current availability of the product (e.g., 'In Stock', 'Out of Stock', 'Limited Stock').

customTag.description: A custom label or badge to display on the product, such as 'HOT', 'NEW', or 'OFFER'.

availability.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the product is currently enabled and available for purchase on the storefront.

categoryId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was first added to the inventory.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the product's details were updated.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if the product should be highlighted on the homepage or in special sections as a featured item.

viewCount.description: A counter for how many times the product's detail page has been viewed, useful for trending products.

purchaseCount.description: A counter for the total number of times this product has been purchased.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase transaction, containing general order information and the customer's contact details at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. This field is null if the order was placed by a guest user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

customerName.description: The full name of the customer who placed the order.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The phone number provided by the customer for this order.

customerAddress.description: The address provided by the customer for this order.

customerEmail.description: The email address provided by the customer for this order.

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific instructions provided by the customer with the order.

orderTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was successfully placed.

totalAmount.description: The calculated total monetary amount of the entire order, including all items and applicable taxes/fees.

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

discordWebhookSent.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the order data has been successfully transmitted to the configured Discord webhook.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product line item within a customer's order, capturing product details specific to that purchase instance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Product being ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

productName.description: The name of the product at the time the order was placed (denormalized to preserve historical accuracy).

productPrice.description: The price of the product at the time the order was placed (denormalized).

quantity.description: The quantity of this specific product included in the order line item.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Stores administrative announcements (e.g., new redeem codes, discounts) to be displayed prominently on the website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

title.description: The title or headline of the announcement.

content.description: The full text content or body of the announcement.

postedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the announcement was officially posted.

isActive.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the announcement is currently active and should be displayed on the website.

definition.description: Stores private profile information for each user. Access is restricted to the user matching the '{userId}' wildcard, which corresponds to 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Stores product categories for organization and filtering. This collection is publicly readable, with write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a product category.

definition.description: Stores details for all digital gaming products available for sale. This collection is publicly readable, with write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a product.

definition.description: Stores customer purchase transactions. If placed by a logged-in user, includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence. Accessible by administrators or the owning user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for an order.

definition.description: Represents individual product line items within a specific order. Includes a denormalized 'userId' from its parent order for authorization independence. Accessible by administrators or the owning user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for an item within the order.

definition.description: Stores administrative announcements. Publicly readable (when 'isActive' is true), with write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for an announcement.

definition.description: A marker collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that the user identified by '{userId}' possesses administrative privileges. No fields beyond the 'id' (which is the userId) are strictly necessary.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of a user designated as an administrator (Firebase Authentication UID).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Castro Nepal is designed with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. The structure leverages path-based ownership, structural segregation, and strategic denormalization to achieve robust authorization independence and enable efficient, queryable authorization patterns (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored at `/user_profiles/{userId}`. Authorization is entirely path-based; `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` wildcard. This eliminates the need for any `get()` operations within security rules, ensuring atomic read/write operations for user profiles.
2.  **Order:** Each order document at `/orders/{orderId}` includes a denormalized `userId` field (if the order was placed by a logged-in user). This `userId` directly links the order to the authenticated user. Security rules can check `request.auth.uid == order.userId` without performing an expensive `get()` on a parent `UserProfile` or relying on potentially non-atomic joins.
3.  **OrderItem:** Documents within `/orders/{orderId}/items/{orderItemId}` also denormalize the `userId` from their parent `Order`. This critical denormalization allows security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == orderItem.userId` directly on the `OrderItem` document, making authorization for subcollections independent of the parent `Order`'s existence or data. This enables atomic creation/deletion of `OrderItem`s relative to their `Order`.
4.  **Public Collections (Category, Product, Announcement):** These collections are designed for public read access (with additional filters like `isActive` for announcements). Their security rules do not require any `get()` operations, as access is determined by global permissions or simple document field checks.
5.  **Admin Roles:** Administrator status is determined by the existence of a document in the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. This 'existence over content' pattern is inherently atomic and avoids `get()` calls for role checks.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  **Public Collections (`/categories`, `/products`, `/announcements`):** These collections maintain a homogeneous security posture (public read access for all documents, or active documents for announcements). Security rules permit `list` operations without requiring specific client-side filters, as all documents in these collections (or the relevant subset) are intended to be publicly viewable. This aligns perfectly with QAPs by ensuring that `list` queries are not rejected due to complex rule-based filtering requirements.
2.  **User-Specific Data (`/user_profiles/{userId}`):** This collection is inherently restricted to specific document IDs based on `request.auth.uid`, making `list` operations irrelevant and `get` operations directly queryable by the authenticated user's ID.
3.  **User-Owned Data (`/orders`, `/orders/{orderId}/items`):** For authenticated users, security rules for `/orders` and `/orders/{orderId}/items` will *mandate* that any client-side `list` query *must* include a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause. The denormalized `userId` in both `Order` and `OrderItem` documents facilitates this pattern. This ensures that users can only list and access their own specific orders and order items, directly enforcing QAPs by requiring client-side filters that correspond to the server-side authorization logic. Administrators, identified by the `/roles_admin/{userId}` marker, will have unrestricted `list` access to all documents in these collections, bypassing the `userId` filter requirement.

This architecture significantly simplifies security rules, enhances performance by minimizing `get()` calls, and provides clear, predictable access patterns for both single document reads and list queries.
